en: What shall we say then? Are we to continue in sin that grace may abound?
zh: 這樣，我們可以說甚麼呢？我們可以常在罪中，叫恩典增多嗎？

en: By no means! How can we who died to sin still live in it?
zh: 絕對不可！我們向罪死了的人，怎麼可以仍然活在罪中呢？

en: Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
zh: 難道你們不曉得我們這受洗歸入基督耶穌的人，是受洗歸入他的死嗎？

en: We were buried therefore with him by baptism into death, in order that, just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, we too might walk in newness of life.
zh: 所以我們藉著洗禮歸入死，與他同葬，為的是要我們過新生命的生活，像基督藉著父的榮耀從死人中復活一樣。

en: For if we have been united with him in a death like his, we shall certainly be united with him in a resurrection like his.
zh: 我們若在他死的樣式上與他聯合，也要在他復活的樣式上與他聯合。

en: We know that our old self was crucified with him in order that the body of sin might be brought to nothing, so that we would no longer be enslaved to sin.
zh: 我們知道，我們的舊人已經與基督同釘十字架，使罪身喪失機能，使我們不再作罪的奴僕，

en: For one who has died has been set free from sin.
zh: 因為死了的人已經脫離罪了。

en: Now if we have died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him.
zh: 我們既然與基督同死，就信也必與他同活。

en: We know that Christ being raised from the dead will never die again; death no longer has dominion over him.
zh: 我們知道，基督既然從死人中復活，就不再死，死也不再轄制他了。

en: For the death he died he died to sin, once for all, but the life he lives he lives to God.
zh: 他死，是向罪死了，只有這一次；他活，是向 神活著。

en: So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus.
zh: 你們也應當這樣，向罪算自己是死的，在基督耶穌裡，向 神卻是活的。

en: Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal bodies, to make you obey their passions.
zh: 所以，不要容罪在你們必死的肉身上掌權，使你們順從肉身的私慾，

en: Do not present your members to sin as instruments for unrighteousness, but present yourselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life, and your members to God as instruments for righteousness.
zh: 也不要把你們的肢體獻給罪，作不義的用具；倒要像出死得生的人，把自己獻給 神，並且把你們的肢體獻給 神作義的用具。

en: For sin will have no dominion over you, since you are not under law but under grace.
zh: 罪必不能轄制你們，因為你們不是在律法之下，而是在恩典之下。

en: What then? Are we to sin because we are not under law but under grace? By no means!
zh: 那卻怎麼樣呢？我們不在律法之下，而在恩典之下，就可以犯罪嗎？絕對不可！

en: Do you not know that if you present yourselves to anyone as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one whom you obey, either of sin, which leads to death, or of obedience, which leads to righteousness?
zh: 難道你們不知道你們自願作奴僕去順從人，順從誰就作誰的奴僕，或作罪的奴僕以致於死，或作順從的奴僕以致於義嗎？

en: But thanks be to God, that you who were once slaves of sin have become obedient from the heart to the standard of teaching to which you were committed,
zh: 感謝 神，你們雖然作過罪的奴僕，現在卻從心裡順從了傳授給你們的教義的規範。

en: and, having been set free from sin, have become slaves of righteousness.
zh: 你們既然從罪裡得了釋放，就作了義的奴僕。

en: I am speaking in human terms, because of your natural limitations. For just as you once presented your members as slaves to impurity and to lawlessness leading to more lawlessness, so now present your members as slaves to righteousness leading to sanctification.
zh: 因為你們肉體的弱點，我就按一般人的話來說，你們從前怎樣把你們的肢體獻給不潔和不法作奴僕，以致於不法，現在也要照樣把你們的肢體獻給義作奴僕，以致於成聖。

en: When you were slaves of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness.
zh: 你們作罪的奴僕的時候，就不受義的約束。

en: But what fruit were you getting at that time from the things of which you are now ashamed? The end of those things is death.
zh: 那麼，你們在現今以為羞恥的事上，當時得了甚麼呢？那些事的結局就是死。

en: But now that you have been set free from sin and have become slaves of God, the fruit you get leads to sanctification and its end, eternal life.
zh: 現在你們既然從罪裡得了釋放，作了 神的奴僕，就有成聖的果子，那結局就是永生。

en: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
zh: 因為罪的工價就是死，但 神的恩賞，在我們的主基督耶穌裡，卻是永生。
